name: Almond Milk (Non-dairy!)
recipeInstructions:
1. Use raw almonds.
2. Place almonds in a blender and grind to a fine powder.
3. Add syrup and 1 cup of water to the blender. Blend for 1-2 minutes until smooth.
4. With the blender running on high, slowly add the remaining water. Blend for 2 minutes.
5. Place a strainer over a large bowl. Line with cheesecloth (optional, but recommended). Otherwise, strain twice using a very fine strainer.
6. Slowly pour the almond milk through the strainer into the bowl.
7. When all of the milk has passed through the strainer, press the remaining milk out of the accumulated almond fiber.
8. Each serving will be approximately 1-1/4 cups. The milk will keep in the fridge for 4-5 days. Leftover fiber can be used as a moisturizing body scrub in the shower.
